整套首页
unit_08 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_08

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 457 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
For feminist scholars, Kessler-Harris's concern that the term "labor history" (i)__________ images of male workers exclusively may be (ii)__________; her own immense contributions to the field offer evidence that feminism has (iii)__________ the questions posed by labor historians, moving women's concerns from the periphery closer to the center of labor history writing.For feminist scholars, Kessler-Harris's concern that the term "labor history" (i)__________ images of male workers exclusively may be (ii)__________; her own immense contributions to the field offer evidence that feminism has (iii)__________ the questions posed by labor historians, moving women's concerns from the periphery closer to the center of labor history writing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　evokes; too pessimistic; altered
underscorespurnoverly
underscore: standard unit headword | spurn: standard unit headword | overly: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Folmer’s book on Edith Wharton seems far removed from recent trends in literary criticism; this need not to be a fault, except that, in its title and introduction, the book (i)__________ to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very (ii)__________ approach.Folmer’s book on Edith Wharton seems far removed from recent trends in literary criticism; this need not to be a fault, except that, in its title and introduction, the book (i)__________ to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very (ii)__________ approach.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　purports; old-fashioned
criticismconversantpurport
criticism: standard unit headword | conversant: standard unit headword | purport: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　revel in; deliberative; defies
ideologyargumentself-serving
ideology: standard unit headword | argument: standard unit headword | self-serving: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
The economist argued that however much the government might trumpet the value of __________, it had been as bold as any other in its spending programs.The economist argued that however much the government might trumpet the value of __________, it had been as bold as any other in its spending programs.
答案：A　thrift
trumpetargue
trumpet: standard unit headword | argue: standard unit headword
#5 SE
Although many skeptics of the scientific theory __________ critiques that have long since been disproved, some of the doubters arguably bring up valid points.Although many skeptics of the scientific theory __________ critiques that have long since been disproved, some of the doubters arguably bring up valid points.
答案：C, F　recycle; rehash
arguablycritique
arguably: standard unit headword | critique: standard unit headword
#6 TC SINGLE
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis
synthesisaccretion
synthesis: standard unit headword | accretion: standard unit headword
#7 SE
The union states its position polemically; its leaders say they are fighting to save good jobs while __________ corporations replace full-time workers with part-time ones in order to cut down on both wages and benefits.The union states its position polemically; its leaders say they are fighting to save good jobs while __________ corporations replace full-time workers with part-time ones in order to cut down on both wages and benefits.
答案：C, F　rapacious; avaricious
enterpriseenterprising
enterprise: standard unit headword | enterprising: standard unit headword
#8 TC TRIPLE
Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder
deferencebumbling
deference: standard unit headword | bumbling: standard unit headword

Far from (i) __________ corporate influence on the academy, Taylor would like to see more of it; he is particularly (ii) __________ the idea of universities partnering with for-profit companies to sell online courses.Far from (i) __________ corporate influence on the academy, Taylor would like to see more of it; he is particularly (ii) __________ the idea of universities partnering with for-profit companies to sell online courses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　decrying; enamored of
decryunnerve
decry: standard unit headword | unnerve: standard unit headword
#10 SE
Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes __________ evidence.Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes __________ evidence.
答案：C, D　untethered from; divorced from
constrictconstrain
constrict: standard unit headword | constrain: standard unit headword
#11 TC SINGLE
A significant element of the Gothic genre, the literary grotesque is sometimes inaccurately discussed as if it were a __________ Gothic or, conversely, as if it were something entirely different from Gothic.A significant element of the Gothic genre, the literary grotesque is sometimes inaccurately discussed as if it were a __________ Gothic or, conversely, as if it were something entirely different from Gothic.
答案：A　synonym for
counterweightramification
counterweight: standard unit headword | ramification: standard unit headword
#12 TC TRIPLE
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce
slumpatypical
slump: standard unit headword | atypical: standard unit headword
#13 TC SINGLE
Alpers and Baxandall remark that Tiepolo is not a recondite painter but rather is quite __________ even to the uninitiated.Alpers and Baxandall remark that Tiepolo is not a recondite painter but rather is quite __________ even to the uninitiated.
答案：B　accessible
prolificimpenetrable
prolific: standard unit headword | impenetrable: standard unit headword
#14 TC SINGLE
The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.
答案：D　flummery
consequentialcalumny
consequential: standard unit headword | calumny: standard unit headword
#15 SE
The theory that the young Mercury collided with another planet appears __________ given Mercury's abundance of volatile elements: a collision would have boiled such elements away.The theory that the young Mercury collided with another planet appears __________ given Mercury's abundance of volatile elements: a collision would have boiled such elements away.
答案：A, D　tenuous; improbable
volatileimprobable
volatile: standard unit headword | improbable: standard unit headword
#16 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
argumentativecriticismvision
argumentative: standard unit headword | criticism: standard unit headword | vision: standard unit headword
#17 TC TRIPLE
Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell
donorfastidious
donor: standard unit headword | fastidious: standard unit headword
#18 TC DOUBLE
The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)__________ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) __________ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)__________ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) __________ as the articles themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative
criticalcriticism
critical: standard unit headword | criticism: standard unit headword

For example, Dutt is best known for creating effects with natural light, but in these galleries this light is purposefully (ii) __________, as protection of the fragile exhibits is a primary design (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　defer to; excluded; goal

Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii) __________, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　uncritical joy; unfounded
besetargument

It is surprising, given the exquisite __________ of its arguments, that the central thesis of A Theory of Justice can be stated so simply.It is surprising, given the exquisite __________ of its arguments, that the central thesis of A Theory of Justice can be stated so simply.
答案：B, D　nuance; subtlety
argumentthesis
argument: standard unit headword | thesis: standard unit headword

As he has matured as a scholar, Felmar has come to see the merit of qualification. His conclusions, which early in his career he (i)__________, are now often (ii)__________.As he has matured as a scholar, Felmar has come to see the merit of qualification. His conclusions, which early in his career he (i)__________, are now often (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stated as absolute; hedged
hedge
hedge: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　anticipates; prefigures

答案：B　a neutrality
affect

Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)__________.Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)__________ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　manipulation; routine

Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to __________: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to __________: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?
答案：E　exculpation
exculpation

答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque

Blank 2
答案：B, E　aimlessness; discursions

Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain

答案：E, F　yield; engender

Putting an old-fashioned disk and letting it play to the end restores a measure of (ii) __________.

答案：B, F　taxing; sanity

One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

Blank 3

答案：A　superficial

Blank 2

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　shaped by; condemn; salutary

Blank 2

Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. Blank 2

He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii) __________ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii) __________ manner he presents in this film.

Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)__________ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)__________ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. According to linguists and cognitive scientists, the human mind specializes in producing analogies. Part of the reason cognitive scientists believe analogy is a (i) __________ cognitive process is that drawing analogies requires some sort of similarity mapping of one concept onto another, and similarity mapping is a component of the inductive thinking we depend on. Another argument for the (ii) __________ of analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (iii) __________ language.According to linguists and cognitive scientists, the human mind specializes in producing analogies. Part of the reason cognitive scientists believe analogy is a (i) __________ cognitive process is that drawing analogies requires some sort of similarity mapping of one concept onto another, and similarity mapping is a component of the inductive thinking we depend on. Another argument for the (ii) __________ of analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (iii) __________ language.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　fundamental; centrality; pervasive in
argumentembed
argument: standard unit headword | embed: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
The company suffers from an almost total lack of __________: even the most innocuous communications between departments tend to devolve into acrimony.The company suffers from an almost total lack of __________: even the most innocuous communications between departments tend to devolve into acrimony.
答案：C　comity
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are __________, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments to medical researchers have tried to show.Science is arguably a very high-minded pursuit, but that is not to say that all of its practitioners are __________, as numerous articles alleging overly generous pharmaceutical industry payments to medical researchers have tried to show.
答案：C, E　unimpeachable; blameless
arguablyoverly
arguably: standard unit headword | overly: standard unit headword

Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievements of the ESA in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievements of the ESA in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain
duplicate
duplicate: standard unit headword

Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an anonymous; confessional

答案：C　an animadversion

Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)__________. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)__________. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal

In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.The author argued that the field of sociology has been overly (i) __________, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism
argueoverly
argue: standard unit headword | overly: standard unit headword

When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.
答案：E, F　defied; disregarded

argument: standard unit headword
#112 TC TRIPLE
Keith Haring's cartoonish art became even more (i)__________ than Andy Warhol's much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved the souvenirs; the critics (ii)__________ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)__________ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring's cartoonish art became even more (i)__________ than Andy Warhol's much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved the souvenirs; the critics (ii)__________ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)__________ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere effort

答案：E　unproven

In what seems a self-conscious (ii) __________ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.

答案：C, F　trivial; inconsequential

His conclusions, which early in his career he (i) __________, are now often (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：A, D　stated as absolutes; hedged

答案：C　complacency

Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.

Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　breakdown; promote; undermining

The fierce drought that is gripping the area--and imminent rationing and steep water price increases--is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.The fierce drought that is gripping the area--and imminent rationing and steep water price increases--is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.
答案：C, E　highlighting; underscoring

答案：A　sophist

In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from

#125 TC DOUBLE

That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　fully vested in them; vitiated

The Golden Gate Bridge has been quite (i)__________ by most aesthetic and functional criteria. However, as a structure it has had some limitations. Shortly after it opened, its roadway proved to be (ii)__________ under certain wind conditions, so it was stiffened. The additional steel that provided that stiffening naturally added weight to the structure, and this made it (iii)__________ later to add a proposed rail system beneath the roadway.The Golden Gate Bridge has been quite (i)__________ by most aesthetic and functional criteria. However, as a structure it has had some limitations. Shortly after it opened, its roadway proved to be (ii)__________ under certain wind conditions, so it was stiffened. The additional steel that provided that stiffening naturally added weight to the structure, and this made it (iii)__________ later to add a proposed rail system beneath the roadway.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　successful; overly flexible; impossible

overly: standard unit headword

Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous

答案：B　Arbitrariness

答案：C, D　novel; original

Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

答案：D　variety

答案：D, F　placate; appease

These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been, too a large extent, (i)__________. These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. #155 TC TRIPLE
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity
vision
vision: standard unit headword
#156 TC DOUBLE
While offering important insights into supernova remnants, Finch's paper is often rather (i) __________: it is (ii) __________ unexplained concepts and details.While offering important insights into supernova remnants, Finch's paper is often rather (i) __________: it is (ii) __________ unexplained concepts and details.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　impenetrable; replete with
impenetrable
impenetrable: standard unit headword

While so-called bioplastics have the potential to significantly reduce our dependence on fossil fuels, they're not __________: claims about bioplastics being biodegradable and compostable are greatly exaggerated.While so-called bioplastics have the potential to significantly reduce our dependence on fossil fuels, they're not __________: claims about bioplastics being biodegradable and compostable are greatly exaggerated.
答案：B　a panacea
fuel
fuel: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable
critical

Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　suffer reputational losses; tainted; a counterweight to
counterweight
counterweight: standard unit headword

答案：E　a palliative to
sensory
sensory: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hews to; lose public support

答案：D, F　brevity; economy

The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)__________ and (ii)__________, prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)__________, or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)__________ and (ii)__________, prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)__________, or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

答案：C　prudent

答案：B, E　confound; perplex

Therefore, a complete lack of any (i) __________, human emotions in ancestral species is (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：B, F　antecedents of; improbable

答案：B　sacrificing rigor

Blank 2
答案：B, E　obviates; perpetuate

Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

答案：A, F　amplify; strengthen

答案：D　variegated

She claimed that the (ii)__________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting women and children in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i)__________ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. She claimed that the (ii)__________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting women and children in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　dire; worsening positions of
argue

Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; accurate; impugned

Indeed, placing consciousness in a general neurophysiological schema of the mind (ii) __________ Freud's entire conceptual understanding of the psyche.

Blank 2

答案：A　an aesthetic
argument

答案：B, F　perspicacity; discernment

One of his favorite examples is inflationary cosmology, which, according to him, is treated as an established theory despite a lack of evidence.

Blank 3

Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)__________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)__________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.

Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking

Blank 2
Blank 3

trade

答案：C, D　purposefulness; design

Biographer John Richardson cautions that Picasso's relationship with Surrealism is easily (i) __________. He stresses that, unlike the Surrealists', Picasso's art was always rooted in some concrete reality, no matter how unreal the imagery may seem; even his most (ii) __________ pictures are representations of the people in his life.Biographer John Richardson cautions that Picasso's relationship with Surrealism is easily (i) __________. He stresses that, unlike the Surrealists', Picasso's art was always rooted in some concrete reality, no matter how unreal the imagery may seem; even his most (ii) __________ pictures are representations of the people in his life.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　overstated; hallucinatory
overstate

#187 TC TRIPLE
The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)__________. These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)__________. These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　self-aggrandizing; value; a product

arguably: standard unit headword
#188 TC SINGLE
Apocalyptic ideology can be __________ in its effects, since those who predict the end of the world have often been great innovators: the fear of catastrophe, despite its perceived inevitability, frequently acts as a spur to construction and renewal.Apocalyptic ideology can be __________ in its effects, since those who predict the end of the world have often been great innovators: the fear of catastrophe, despite its perceived inevitability, frequently acts as a spur to construction and renewal.
答案：C　paradoxical

答案：B, D　fastidious; finicky

Blank 2

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population declines that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population declines that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
affect

Blank 2

答案：B　conjectural

#194 SE
The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through __________ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through __________ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.
答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.

Blank 3

As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.

The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.

Blank 2

And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, E　demanded; exacted

答案：D　boast of

Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.

Blank 2

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　platitudinous; innocuous; conciliatory
critiqueinnocuous
critique: standard unit headword | innocuous: standard unit headword
#207 SE
Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#208 TC SINGLE
The artists’ career was remarkable partly because it was so __________: she died, with only a few paintings to her credit, while still in her twenties.The artists’ career was remarkable partly because it was so __________: she died, with only a few paintings to her credit, while still in her twenties.
答案：E　abbreviated
prolific
prolific: standard unit headword
#209 TC DOUBLE
In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i) __________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii) __________ a judge's ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i) __________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii) __________ a judge's ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　promotes; constrains
argumentconstrain
argument: standard unit headword | constrain: standard unit headword
#210 SE
Although a dedicated reader of the book can __________ a brief chronology of Chappell's life, the book is nevertheless not a real biography of Chappell.Although a dedicated reader of the book can __________ a brief chronology of Chappell's life, the book is nevertheless not a real biography of Chappell.
答案：B, F　obtain; glean
brief
brief: standard unit headword
#211 TC TRIPLE
Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt
fastidious
fastidious: standard unit headword
#212 TC DOUBLE
The enlightenment philosophers of the eighteenth century acknowledged that unrestricted freedom to publish could (i) __________ motivated by envy or hatred, but they hoped that any such (ii) __________ enabled by expanded liberties would suffer from being publicly exposed.The enlightenment philosophers of the eighteenth century acknowledged that unrestricted freedom to publish could (i) __________ motivated by envy or hatred, but they hoped that any such (ii) __________ enabled by expanded liberties would suffer from being publicly exposed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　facilitate calumny; defamation

calumny: standard unit headword | constrain: standard unit headword

In his youth the naturalist and artist James Audubon was given to __________ glamorous tales about himself: he falsely claimed to have studied under a renowned French painter and hinted that he was the heir apparent to the French throne.In his youth the naturalist and artist James Audubon was given to __________ glamorous tales about himself: he falsely claimed to have studied under a renowned French painter and hinted that he was the heir apparent to the French throne.
答案：E　concocting

constrain: standard unit headword
#214 TC TRIPLE
Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#215 SE
Rosen argues that in the early years of the United States, individual states played important roles in the governance of American Indians, thereby __________ the common assumption that Indian policy was exclusively the federal government's domain.Rosen argues that in the early years of the United States, individual states played important roles in the governance of American Indians, thereby __________ the common assumption that Indian policy was exclusively the federal government's domain.
答案：B, D　belying; contradicting
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#216 TC TRIPLE
The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　degree of gruesome detail; sensationalism; underscoring
deferenceunderscore
deference: standard unit headword | underscore: standard unit headword
#217 TC SINGLE
Although not enough to __________ the conventional view of the manuscript’s provenance, the new study was thought to have weakened the prevailing theory considerably.Although not enough to __________ the conventional view of the manuscript’s provenance, the new study was thought to have weakened the prevailing theory considerably.
答案：E　topple
affect
affect: standard unit headword
#218 TC DOUBLE
Architecture scholar Sandy McCreery recently argued that traffic congestion, far from being a sign of urban (i) __________, is a mark of urban (ii) __________; congestion promotes contemplation of our surroundings and provides us with a shared experience, thereby fulfilling the essential task of the city.Architecture scholar Sandy McCreery recently argued that traffic congestion, far from being a sign of urban (i) __________, is a mark of urban (ii) __________; congestion promotes contemplation of our surroundings and provides us with a shared experience, thereby fulfilling the essential task of the city.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　decline; health
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#219 SE
We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not __________ repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not __________ repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.
答案：A, D　spurned; rebuffed

The cognitive flexibility of successful fictional detectives is often __________ by their cultural ambivalence: detectives’ intellectual acumen, it seems, exists in direct proportion to their uneasy place in society.The cognitive flexibility of successful fictional detectives is often __________ by their cultural ambivalence: detectives’ intellectual acumen, it seems, exists in direct proportion to their uneasy place in society.
答案：D　matched
amplify
amplify: standard unit headword
#221 TC DOUBLE

Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking
overly
overly: standard unit headword
#222 TC TRIPLE
The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.The author of this (i)__________ memoir is notable for an unmitigated (ii)__________ that is matched only by an utter lack of self-awareness. Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　self-serving; self-regard; self-aggrandizement

critical: standard unit headword | self-serving: standard unit headword

Garcia's recent rise to prominence has left her feeling conflicted; she is troubled by the __________ of being an avowed outsider who has suddenly become part of the establishment.Garcia's recent rise to prominence has left her feeling conflicted; she is troubled by the __________ of being an avowed outsider who has suddenly become part of the establishment.
答案：A, C　irony; incongruity

avow: standard unit headword

答案：C　unperturbed by

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　supplies; strong bargaining positions

答案：A, D　affecting; moving

The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility

#228 TC SINGLE
In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not __________ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not __________ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.
答案：B　extirpate

Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i) __________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii) __________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.

Blank 2

The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize

Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.

答案：B, D, I　demarcates; apocryphal; senescence

The essential difference between writing nonfiction and fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and fiction is that the artist can (i)__________ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)__________. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)__________ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity

答案：C, F　renounce; disavow

In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i) __________ meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii) __________.A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i) __________ meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii) __________.

Blank 2

The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

#251 SE
The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.
答案：D, E　truculence; pugnacity

答案：C　hinder

Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at macroscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii) __________.

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B, F　collective; social

Blank 2

#258 TC TRIPLE
The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　partially offset by; shrinking; strengthening
fuel
fuel: standard unit headword
#259 SE
Rather than fully embracing a position of __________, Alurista rejects the view that his poetry provides only negative critique; rather, he aims to reconstruct a Chicano consciousness uncolored by views imposed through colonization.Rather than fully embracing a position of __________, Alurista rejects the view that his poetry provides only negative critique; rather, he aims to reconstruct a Chicano consciousness uncolored by views imposed through colonization.
答案：B, E　oppositionality; protest
critique
critique: standard unit headword
#260 TC SINGLE
Although the author has entered a scholarly field that is fraught with bitter partisanship, her book displays a remarkable lack of __________.Although the author has entered a scholarly field that is fraught with bitter partisanship, her book displays a remarkable lack of __________.
答案：C　vitriol
deference
deference: standard unit headword
#261 TC DOUBLE
In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i) __________ and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii) __________ learning of more sophisticated understanding. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i) __________ and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii) __________ learning of more sophisticated understanding. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unproductive; foster
overly
overly: standard unit headword
#262 TC TRIPLE
With so many scientific papers chasing so few pages in the most prestigious journals, the winners could be the ones most likely to oversell themselves - to trumpet amazing or important results that later turn out to be false. This would produce a (i)__________ picture of scientific knowledge, with less (ii)__________ but more (iii)__________ results either relegated to obscure journals or left unpublished.With so many scientific papers chasing so few pages in the most prestigious journals, the winners could be the ones most likely to oversell themselves - to trumpet amazing or important results that later turn out to be false. This would produce a (i)__________ picture of scientific knowledge, with less (ii)__________ but more (iii)__________ results either relegated to obscure journals or left unpublished.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　distorted; dramatic; accurate
trumpet
trumpet: standard unit headword
#263 SE
Brief personal impressions are rarely __________, especially when recalled after many years; but the author's initial impression of Gellner as an exceptionally honest thinker is beyond reasonable doubt.Brief personal impressions are rarely __________, especially when recalled after many years; but the author's initial impression of Gellner as an exceptionally honest thinker is beyond reasonable doubt.
答案：B, E　definitive; authoritative
brief
brief: standard unit headword
#264 TC SINGLE
The book's approach to modern art was hardly __________: it aimed simply to give readers a deeper understanding of prevailing perspectives in the field.The book's approach to modern art was hardly __________: it aimed simply to give readers a deeper understanding of prevailing perspectives in the field.
答案：A　revisionist
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#265 TC DOUBLE
Shirky argues that the Internet (i) __________ the needs for hierarchical structures and the sluggish organizations that (ii) __________ them: the Internet makes it possible to do things cheaply and efficiently on one's own.Shirky argues that the Internet (i) __________ the needs for hierarchical structures and the sluggish organizations that (ii) __________ them: the Internet makes it possible to do things cheaply and efficiently on one's own.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　obviates; perpetuate
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#266 TC TRIPLE
Some chemists argue that although molecular motors are (i)__________, they are (ii)__________ by themselves; nevertheless, the chemical principles behind them might be (iii)__________: using the same light-activated mechanism that drives certain molecular motors, researchers have developed around 100 drug-like compounds that can be switched on or off in response to light.Some chemists argue that although molecular motors are (i)__________, they are (ii)__________ by themselves; nevertheless, the chemical principles behind them might be (iii)__________: using the same light-activated mechanism that drives certain molecular motors, researchers have developed around 100 drug-like compounds that can be switched on or off in response to light.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　appealing; useless; highly valuable
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#267 SE
Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.
答案：C, F　prudent; circumspect
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#268 TC SINGLE
The physical act of drinking may seem __________ to humans since we can fully close our mouths to create suction, but species that cannot do so, including most adult carnivores, must resort to some other mechanism.The physical act of drinking may seem __________ to humans since we can fully close our mouths to create suction, but species that cannot do so, including most adult carnivores, must resort to some other mechanism.
答案：C　uncomplicated

innocuous: standard unit headword
#269 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; thoroughness

答案：A, F　paucity; scarcity

答案：D　uncomfortable precedent

Blank 1
Blank 2

Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii) __________. And "style" was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii) __________ poor thought.Our contemporary preference for natural expression over artifice is (i) __________ the traditional Western view that the skill with which you expressed a position corresponded closely to the credibility of your argument. Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii) __________. And "style" was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii) __________ poor thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a departure from; indifference; betrayed

While dehydration during pregnancy does not affect aspic vipers' litter size, pregnant females lost 89 percent more mass than non-reproductive females did when water-deprived, suggesting that female snakes may __________ their own water balance to benefit their offspring.While dehydration during pregnancy does not affect aspic vipers' litter size, pregnant females lost 89 percent more mass than non-reproductive females did when water-deprived, suggesting that female snakes may __________ their own water balance to benefit their offspring.
答案：A, D　compromise; sacrifice

A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. 答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive

答案：B, F　enthralled by; dazzled by

Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amenable to refutation; upended by; persisted

答案：A, E　elisions; omissions

She claimed that the (ii) __________ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.

Blank 2

A certain (i) __________ governs the way most of us think about routines. A routine is sometimes conceived as constraining--a self-made prison of inflexible habits that prevent people from changing their lives. But routine is also seen as (ii) __________: activities that become routine (iii) __________, allowing us to expend energy on other things.A certain (i) __________ governs the way most of us think about routines. A routine is sometimes conceived as constraining--a self-made prison of inflexible habits that prevent people from changing their lives. But routine is also seen as (ii) __________: activities that become routine (iii) __________, allowing us to expend energy on other things.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　polarity; liberating; demand less attention

答案：B, D　nebulous; amorphous

That said, Gelles then (ii) __________ to promote Adams from a writer of private letters to a public figure, arguing that she was a significant force for change.Gelles urges readers of Abigail Adams' letters to consider their (i) __________: in Adams' era women were denied a public persona separate from that of their husbands. That said, Gelles then (ii) __________ to promote Adams from a writer of private letters to a public figure, arguing that she was a significant force for change.

Their physiology (iii)__________ their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i)__________ eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii)__________. Their physiology (iii)__________ their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　accustomed to; an unnecessary cost; adapted to

Blank 2

Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　presence; function as a proxy for; supporting

答案：A, B　inspirational; animating

No matter how much input orchestra players are allowed in choosing a music director, they never feel they have enough. Regardless, their criticisms of Webster's expertise and stature within the field are nothing short of (iii) __________. Would they really prefer some of the other potential candidates whose names have been released?It's possible that the (i) __________ of the musicians about the director's appointment is mostly an expression of (ii) __________ the selection process. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) __________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) __________ children whose nature is to be inactive.The study found that every child has his or her own highly (i) __________ daily level of activity. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) __________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) __________ children whose nature is to be inactive.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　consistent; intrinsic; will have little impact on

答案：A, F　eschew; avoid

However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　infer; unusual; differentially

答案：B, D　fortuitous; accidental
