Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored
criticism
criticism: standard unit headword
#310 SE
The awards and critical acclaim for the author's new novel were not unexpected, as her previous works had already secured their author a reputation for literary __________.The awards and critical acclaim for the author's new novel were not unexpected, as her previous works had already secured their author a reputation for literary __________.
答案：B, E　virtuosity; mastery
critical
critical: standard unit headword
#311 TC DOUBLE
Meegan Garber argues that the typical suburban lawn, which was originally designed to (i) __________ homes even as it enforced the distance between them, may be considered (ii) __________ domestic space: the whole neighborhood is invested in the appearance of each neighbor's yard.Meegan Garber argues that the typical suburban lawn, which was originally designed to (i) __________ homes even as it enforced the distance between them, may be considered (ii) __________ domestic space: the whole neighborhood is invested in the appearance of each neighbor's yard.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　connect; common
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#312 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
critical
critical: standard unit headword
#313 SE
Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not __________ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not __________ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with
arguedefer
argue: standard unit headword | defer: standard unit headword
#314 TC DOUBLE
Over the years, some researchers and educators have (i) __________ the argument for hands-on learning of science, (ii) __________ that a more straightforward approach--known as direct instruction--has the potential to help students learn science more effectively.Over the years, some researchers and educators have (i) __________ the argument for hands-on learning of science, (ii) __________ that a more straightforward approach--known as direct instruction--has the potential to help students learn science more effectively.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　challenged; maintaining
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#315 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
enterprise
enterprise: standard unit headword
#316 SE
Readers looking for another condemnation of private equity firms should look elsewhere, for this book is not __________ such firms.Readers looking for another condemnation of private equity firms should look elsewhere, for this book is not __________ such firms.
答案：A, B　a rant against; a diatribe on
exculpationargument
exculpation: standard unit headword | argument: standard unit headword
#317 TC DOUBLE
All of these writers rely on (i) __________ and (ii) __________ to make their argument: they take a single idea and illustrate it with endless, rather overblown examples while avoiding any genuinely nuanced analysis.All of these writers rely on (i) __________ and (ii) __________ to make their argument: they take a single idea and illustrate it with endless, rather overblown examples while avoiding any genuinely nuanced analysis.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　simplification; exaggeration
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#318 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
underscore
underscore: standard unit headword
#319 SE
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
deliberate
deliberate: standard unit headword
#320 TC DOUBLE
Most scientists would argue that consciousness is (i) __________ animals with relatively complex brains. The more scientists study animal behavior and brain anatomy, however, the more it seems that very complex brains are (ii) __________ consciousness.Most scientists would argue that consciousness is (i) __________ animals with relatively complex brains. The more scientists study animal behavior and brain anatomy, however, the more it seems that very complex brains are (ii) __________ consciousness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　restricted to; unnecessary for
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#321 TC TRIPLE
Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　superior to; closer to; apologize for
constrain
constrain: standard unit headword
#322 SE
For all the __________ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.For all the __________ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.
答案：A, B　encomiums; tributes
critique
critique: standard unit headword
#323 TC DOUBLE
The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　inadequate; can emerge too late to protect

argue: standard unit headword
#324 TC TRIPLE
In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#325 SE
Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.
答案：B, E　galvanize; animate
affect
affect: standard unit headword
#326 TC DOUBLE
Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian
argument
argument: standard unit headword
#327 TC TRIPLE
It has been argued that the extinction of megamammals, such as mammoths, had a (i) __________ effect on certain species because megamammals were "keystone herbivore species": their disappearance (ii) __________ natural processes such as tree regeneration and shrub invasions of grassy glades, (iii) __________ nonmigratory grazers that feed on growing grass and pasturage.It has been argued that the extinction of megamammals, such as mammoths, had a (i) __________ effect on certain species because megamammals were "keystone herbivore species": their disappearance (ii) __________ natural processes such as tree regeneration and shrub invasions of grassy glades, (iii) __________ nonmigratory grazers that feed on growing grass and pasturage.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　negative; stimulated; disadvantaging
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#328 SE
Jaime Javier Rodriguez notes that popular art forms often conceal a daunting complexity: frequently what appears __________ becomes intricate and challenging when closely examined.Jaime Javier Rodriguez notes that popular art forms often conceal a daunting complexity: frequently what appears __________ becomes intricate and challenging when closely examined.
答案：B, F　canned; formulaic
tortuous
tortuous: standard unit headword
#329 TC DOUBLE
Boostrom argues that many scholars are too (i) __________ a tendency toward (ii) __________ in academic prose. It may even be, he says, that some academics deliberately befog their subject with in-group jargon in hopes of lending substance to their thinking.Boostrom argues that many scholars are too (i) __________ a tendency toward (ii) __________ in academic prose. It may even be, he says, that some academics deliberately befog their subject with in-group jargon in hopes of lending substance to their thinking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　forgiving of; obfuscation

Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　churlish; approbation; consistently perspicacious

William Perkins, his __________ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.William Perkins, his __________ speaking style notwithstanding, has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.
答案：A, F　fiery; impassioned

Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　indirect; an inference

The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. But what these blunders had in common was that no law of physics, or at least no (iii) __________ one, was invoked to support the claim.This is not the first time science has been invoked to argue for inherent limits to the capabilities of technology. The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. But what these blunders had in common was that no law of physics, or at least no (iii) __________ one, was invoked to support the claim.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　embarrassing; ingenuity; established

Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)__________ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)__________.

答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account

This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. Blank 2

The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)__________ by the German medical system, which (iii)__________ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)__________, from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)__________ by the German medical system, which (iii)__________ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　malady; healthful; funds

They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious

Blank 2

Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence

答案：E, F　tempered; modulated

The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes

#353 TC DOUBLE
In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#354 SE
Upon realizing that the indicators of a stressful situation can be extremely __________, the psychologist reconsidered her claim that a reliable way of reducing stress is to recognize stressful situations and then avoid them.Upon realizing that the indicators of a stressful situation can be extremely __________, the psychologist reconsidered her claim that a reliable way of reducing stress is to recognize stressful situations and then avoid them.
答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous

#355 TC TRIPLE
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i) __________. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors' interests are also becoming more (ii) __________. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii) __________ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i) __________. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors' interests are also becoming more (ii) __________. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii) __________ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive
antiquarian
antiquarian: standard unit headword
#356 TC DOUBLE
My (i) __________ Hansen's life-size sculpture for real people was memorable precisely because the work did (ii) __________ such a complete -- if brief -- deception, one that is exceptional in my experience as a viewer of representational art.My (i) __________ Hansen's life-size sculpture for real people was memorable precisely because the work did (ii) __________ such a complete -- if brief -- deception, one that is exceptional in my experience as a viewer of representational art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　mistaking; effect
brief
brief: standard unit headword

The book captures the __________ of several politicians who spoke publicly of old-time virtues in order to mask private vices.The book captures the __________ of several politicians who spoke publicly of old-time virtues in order to mask private vices.
答案：B, F　dissemblance; duplicity
rectitude
rectitude: standard unit headword
#358 TC TRIPLE
Just because, as a photographer, Friedlander (i)__________ places that most people consider ugly does not mean that he is out to prove they are beautiful. Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)__________ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)__________.Just because, as a photographer, Friedlander (i)__________ places that most people consider ugly does not mean that he is out to prove they are beautiful. Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)__________ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　is interested in; document; aesthetics
critical
critical: standard unit headword
#359 TC DOUBLE
The company is uniquely positioned to (i) __________ the ongoing crisis in the economy because its highly designed products, although relatively expensive, appeal to (ii) __________ consumers, who are less likely to be affected by an economic downturn.The company is uniquely positioned to (i) __________ the ongoing crisis in the economy because its highly designed products, although relatively expensive, appeal to (ii) __________ consumers, who are less likely to be affected by an economic downturn.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　weather; affluent
affect
affect: standard unit headword
#360 SE
The concert hall’s suspended ceiling is two-inch-thick plaster that reflects low-frequency sound energy; similarly, all wall surfaces are angled and shaped to __________ sound from the stage throughout the audience area.The concert hall’s suspended ceiling is two-inch-thick plaster that reflects low-frequency sound energy; similarly, all wall surfaces are angled and shaped to __________ sound from the stage throughout the audience area.
答案：A, C　diffuse; spread
amplify
amplify: standard unit headword
#361 TC TRIPLE
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
atypical
atypical: standard unit headword
#362 TC DOUBLE
The company's chief executive is (i) __________ the desire to postpone action until a consensus has been reached. Instead she makes a brief for instinctive decision making, treating extensive deliberation as something to (ii) __________.The company's chief executive is (i) __________ the desire to postpone action until a consensus has been reached. Instead she makes a brief for instinctive decision making, treating extensive deliberation as something to (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　skeptical of; shun
brief
brief: standard unit headword
#363 SE
After the Turkish Republic was established traditional hamars (bathhouses) seemed to many Turks to be outmoded, but thanks to tourism, hamers have experienced a __________, becoming important cultural sites for foreign and Turkish visitors alike.After the Turkish Republic was established traditional hamars (bathhouses) seemed to many Turks to be outmoded, but thanks to tourism, hamers have experienced a __________, becoming important cultural sites for foreign and Turkish visitors alike.
答案：D, F　revival; renaissance
slump
slump: standard unit headword

The historian's narrative of the dynasty's decline is (i)__________, because though there is nothing (ii)__________ the account, largely parts of it rely on (iii)__________.The historian's narrative of the dynasty's decline is (i)__________, because though there is nothing (ii)__________ the account, largely parts of it rely on (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　questionable; inherently implausible about; conjecture

penetrating: standard unit headword

Fallows argues that America's two-and-a-half centuries of rapid economic growth, rather than portending endless, similarly paced future advances, have amounted to harvesting the "low-hanging fruit" of open land, cheap energy, and industrial-era breakthroughs. Thus, such growth has been (i) __________ episode that is unlikely to be (ii) __________.Fallows argues that America's two-and-a-half centuries of rapid economic growth, rather than portending endless, similarly paced future advances, have amounted to harvesting the "low-hanging fruit" of open land, cheap energy, and industrial-era breakthroughs. Thus, such growth has been (i) __________ episode that is unlikely to be (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　a unique; sustained
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#366 SE
The 1840s were __________ time for young women beginning to study science, particularly astronomy, in 1847 Maria Mitchell discovered the Nantucket comet, the first of several important astronomical discoveries of the era.The 1840s were __________ time for young women beginning to study science, particularly astronomy, in 1847 Maria Mitchell discovered the Nantucket comet, the first of several important astronomical discoveries of the era.
答案：A, F　a favorable; an auspicious
improbable
improbable: standard unit headword
#367 TC TRIPLE
To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)__________ the United States, but his (ii)__________ was stemmed not from his (iii)__________ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)__________ the United States, but his (ii)__________ was stemmed not from his (iii)__________ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　stranger in; alienation; birthplace

#368 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intelligibility; essential
arguably
arguably: standard unit headword
#369 SE
Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually __________ the gathering of important data.Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually __________ the gathering of important data.
答案：B, C　expedite; facilitate

affect: standard unit headword
#370 TC TRIPLE
The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science--even "pure" science--can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be: scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science--even "pure" science--can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be: scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes

argument: standard unit headword

Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; opprobrium

For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)__________ from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)__________ by the German medical system, which (iii)__________ some of these treatments. Consequently, the treatments are widely used.For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)__________ from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)__________ by the German medical system, which (iii)__________ some of these treatments. Consequently, the treatments are widely used.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　malady; healthful; funds

innocuous: standard unit headword

The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.
答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught

sordid: standard unit headword
#374 TC DOUBLE
The bankruptcy expert has long argued that the entrepreneurial spirit in the United States is (i) __________ the practice that we, as a society, have had of treating risk takers (ii) __________ when they fail. Reweighting the system to favor creditors could diminish that spirit, she warns, and make it harder for entrepreneurs to try again after a failure.The bankruptcy expert has long argued that the entrepreneurial spirit in the United States is (i) __________ the practice that we, as a society, have had of treating risk takers (ii) __________ when they fail. Reweighting the system to favor creditors could diminish that spirit, she warns, and make it harder for entrepreneurs to try again after a failure.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　fostered by; charitably

These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.

答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at

答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous

Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.

It is filled with references to some people whose ideas seemed (ii)__________ at that time, but are rarely viewed as (iii)__________ persons now.To many newspaper readers now, this book published in 1953 is (i)__________. It is filled with references to some people whose ideas seemed (ii)__________ at that time, but are rarely viewed as (iii)__________ persons now.

答案：B, E　transitory; ephemeral

Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory

Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)__________ raw life? He was as vivid physically as he was mentally, almost perversely alert, completely at home in the world of matter, repulsed by (iii)__________.Saul's particular combination of intellectuality and vitality was not paradoxical; it was category-shattering. (i)__________ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)__________ raw life? Blank 2

Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)__________ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii)__________ on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)__________ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)__________ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. 答案：B, D, I　significant filters to; vaporize; common

Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)__________ reception that academics - motivated partly by (iii)__________ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards - generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　inverse; scornful; snobbish elitism

Amelia Earhart, Freya Stark, and Gertrude Bell may be (ii)__________, but only a specialist would recognize the names of Fanny Workman or Janet Wulsin. prolific: standard unit headword
#404 TC DOUBLE
Far from (i) __________ the actions taken by the newspaper's executives, William praised the executive's resistance to corruption--yet he doubted that their policies were practical enough to warrant (ii) __________ by other papers.Far from (i) __________ the actions taken by the newspaper's executives, William praised the executive's resistance to corruption--yet he doubted that their policies were practical enough to warrant (ii) __________ by other papers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　impugning; emulation
criticism
criticism: standard unit headword
#405 TC TRIPLE
The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　accelerated; at the same rate; self-evident
constrain
constrain: standard unit headword
#406 SE
One of the reporter's central themes is that health care concerns were a secondary consideration to the reformers in their drive to limit if not completely __________ the consumption of cigarettes.One of the reporter's central themes is that health care concerns were a secondary consideration to the reformers in their drive to limit if not completely __________ the consumption of cigarettes.
答案：B, E　proscribe; disallow
spurn
spurn: standard unit headword
#407 TC DOUBLE
Though she had some exposure to great art and high culture, it must be said the ultra athletic Marion Carstairs remained throughout her life primarily (i) __________ mental and artistic pursuits. She was by nature (ii) __________.Though she had some exposure to great art and high culture, it must be said the ultra athletic Marion Carstairs remained throughout her life primarily (i) __________ mental and artistic pursuits. She was by nature (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　indifferent to; neither bookish nor cerebral
argumentative
argumentative: standard unit headword
#408 TC TRIPLE
For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　accessible; proliferation of detail; oversimplifying
ornate
ornate: standard unit headword
#409 SE
The beauty of popular fiction always lies in some __________ conviction, a writer's faith in courage, honor, integrity, love, whatever--a faith that's impossible to fake, difficult even to acquire.The beauty of popular fiction always lies in some __________ conviction, a writer's faith in courage, honor, integrity, love, whatever--a faith that's impossible to fake, difficult even to acquire.
答案：C, F　unreserved; wholehearted
affect
affect: standard unit headword
#410 TC DOUBLE
Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolve; circumvent
amplify
amplify: standard unit headword
#411 TC TRIPLE
For decades scientists believed in the (i) __________ of long-term memories: they were unstable for a few hours but then became etched into the brain for good. Current research, however, suggests that recalling a memory causes it to revert temporarily to an insecure state in which the recollection can be (ii) __________. Thus, memory is much more (iii) __________ than scientists had previously thought it to be.For decades scientists believed in the (i) __________ of long-term memories: they were unstable for a few hours but then became etched into the brain for good. Current research, however, suggests that recalling a memory causes it to revert temporarily to an insecure state in which the recollection can be (ii) __________. Thus, memory is much more (iii) __________ than scientists had previously thought it to be.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　immutability; modified; fluid
constrain
constrain: standard unit headword
#412 SE
The early universe was __________, but no longer: galaxies, galaxy clusters, and stars punctuate the expanse of empty space, staking their uneven distribution throughout the firmament.The early universe was __________, but no longer: galaxies, galaxy clusters, and stars punctuate the expanse of empty space, staking their uneven distribution throughout the firmament.
答案：C, E　uniform; homogeneous
impenetrable
impenetrable: standard unit headword
#413 TC DOUBLE
She knew well, from experience with hundreds of hired crew members on her boats, how (i) __________ attitudes can be: one negative influence can impel an otherwise (ii) __________ member of a crew to quit.She knew well, from experience with hundreds of hired crew members on her boats, how (i) __________ attitudes can be: one negative influence can impel an otherwise (ii) __________ member of a crew to quit.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　infectious; contented
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#414 TC TRIPLE
The latest encyclopedia (i) __________ the (ii) __________ made by many other books on how things work--giving (iii) __________ for every device, no matter how complicated--by taking just as long as is needed to explain things.The latest encyclopedia (i) __________ the (ii) __________ made by many other books on how things work--giving (iii) __________ for every device, no matter how complicated--by taking just as long as is needed to explain things.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　avoids; compromise; an equal number of words
brief
brief: standard unit headword
#415 SE
The __________ markings on some prehistoric bone and antler fragments could be accidents or the result of doodling, but may also represent a form of communication such as tally marks or even early calendars.The __________ markings on some prehistoric bone and antler fragments could be accidents or the result of doodling, but may also represent a form of communication such as tally marks or even early calendars.
答案：B, D　enigmatic; inscrutable
deliberate
deliberate: standard unit headword
#416 TC DOUBLE
Recently the novelist has (i) __________ the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii) __________ narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run wild.Recently the novelist has (i) __________ the radically experimental forms with which he made his reputation in favor of more (ii) __________ narratives, fencing in and turning an imagination meant to run wild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　forsaken; conventional
trumpet
trumpet: standard unit headword
#417 TC TRIPLE
Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　maximize; reducing; added
defer
defer: standard unit headword
#418 SE
Despite the critic's assertion that Willa Cather's place in the literary canon is now so entrenched that it scarcely matters if she were really a modernist, her reputation arguably remains __________.Despite the critic's assertion that Willa Cather's place in the literary canon is now so entrenched that it scarcely matters if she were really a modernist, her reputation arguably remains __________.
答案：B, D　uncertain; tenuous
consequentialarguably
consequential: standard unit headword | arguably: standard unit headword
#419 TC DOUBLE
Many civic institutions tend to (i) __________, when they do not actively discourage, the better natures of the citizenry. People are individually altruistic social animals who nonetheless teach themselves to be (ii) __________ in the public sphere.Many civic institutions tend to (i) __________, when they do not actively discourage, the better natures of the citizenry. People are individually altruistic social animals who nonetheless teach themselves to be (ii) __________ in the public sphere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　ignore; selfish
critical
critical: standard unit headword
#420 TC TRIPLE
Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.Although the Vibrio fischeri bacterium has the capacity to make all the complex nutrients it needs from a single source of carbon and nitrogen, scientists believe that if V. fischeri is in an environment that (i) __________ particular nutrients, it will use the nutrients in the environment first. This bacterium, in other words, prefers (ii) __________ to making its own meals from scratch, the (iii) __________ approach being more energy efficient and allowing the bacterium to grow much faster.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　affords; getting take-out food; former
synthesize
synthesize: standard unit headword
#421 TC DOUBLE
Sinden notes that in the face of warnings about the perils of climate change, the need to agree on strategies for reducing greenhouse-gas emissions is increasingly urgent. The question of who should reduce emissions is not (i) __________. Rather, according to Sinden, it is the question of who should bear the costs of these reductions that is (ii) __________.Sinden notes that in the face of warnings about the perils of climate change, the need to agree on strategies for reducing greenhouse-gas emissions is increasingly urgent. The question of who should reduce emissions is not (i) __________. Rather, according to Sinden, it is the question of who should bear the costs of these reductions that is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　particularly vexed; contentious
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#422 TC TRIPLE
Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) __________; they were widely disparaged for being so (ii) __________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) __________.Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) __________; they were widely disparaged for being so (ii) __________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#423 TC DOUBLE
Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Given such a (i)__________ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)__________ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Given such a (i)__________ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)__________ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　cavalier disregard for; ironic
critical
critical: standard unit headword
#424 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
critique
critique: standard unit headword
#425 TC DOUBLE
Ethical theorists often assume that the verb "ought" means roughly "has an obligation." This assumption, however, is (i) __________ the (ii) __________ of "ought" sentences in English: the word is used in too many ways to admit a single definition.Ethical theorists often assume that the verb "ought" means roughly "has an obligation." This assumption, however, is (i) __________ the (ii) __________ of "ought" sentences in English: the word is used in too many ways to admit a single definition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied by; diversity
consequential
consequential: standard unit headword
#426 TC TRIPLE
The literary critic's first book, coming after a long period of reviewing poetry in magazines, is the author's chance to (i) __________ positions previously (ii) __________ somewhat confusingly, and show how they (iii) __________, if in fact they do.The literary critic's first book, coming after a long period of reviewing poetry in magazines, is the author's chance to (i) __________ positions previously (ii) __________ somewhat confusingly, and show how they (iii) __________, if in fact they do.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　clarify; intimated; cohere
duplicate
duplicate: standard unit headword
#427 TC DOUBLE
Insects are often (i) __________ as the world's most versatile and (ii) __________ flying machines. Yet they shouldn't even be able to get off the ground, at least not according to a straightforward interpretation of the laws of aerodynamics.Insects are often (i) __________ as the world's most versatile and (ii) __________ flying machines. Yet they shouldn't even be able to get off the ground, at least not according to a straightforward interpretation of the laws of aerodynamics.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　touted; maneuverable
atypical
atypical: standard unit headword
#428 TC TRIPLE
Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against
argue
argue: standard unit headword
#429 TC DOUBLE
With respect to environmental issues, most legal scholarship tends to envision politics as the pursuit of already fixed interests, a view that (i) __________ the ability of political communities to (ii) __________ their values and their interests through the process of democratic deliberation.With respect to environmental issues, most legal scholarship tends to envision politics as the pursuit of already fixed interests, a view that (i) __________ the ability of political communities to (ii) __________ their values and their interests through the process of democratic deliberation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　ignores; transform
overstate
overstate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　conviction; unreasonable; feel absolute certainty

#431 TC DOUBLE
Especially when poorly organized, alliances among nations can send messages that (i) __________ either credible threats or credible assurances, thus making stable coercive diplomacy with enemies (short of war) (ii) __________ .Especially when poorly organized, alliances among nations can send messages that (i) __________ either credible threats or credible assurances, thus making stable coercive diplomacy with enemies (short of war) (ii) __________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undercut; harder to maintain

defer: standard unit headword
#432 TC TRIPLE
Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.Every few years, as a reviewer, one encounters a novel whose (i) __________ are so many in number, and so (ii) __________, that to correct them fully would produce a text that exceeded the novel itself in length. Faced with such a book, one wishes only to let it slip quietly to the seabed of culture, there to join thousands of other (iii) __________ books in their slow disintegration.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ineptitudes; thoroughgoing; incompetent
innocuous
innocuous: standard unit headword
#433 TC DOUBLE
To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　question the adequacy; conducive to
critique
critique: standard unit headword
#434 TC TRIPLE
While the subtropical ocean gyres are (i) __________ accumulation zones of floating plastic debris, the possibility of plastic accumulation at polar latitudes has been (ii) __________ because of the (iii) __________ of nearby pollution sources.While the subtropical ocean gyres are (i) __________ accumulation zones of floating plastic debris, the possibility of plastic accumulation at polar latitudes has been (ii) __________ because of the (iii) __________ of nearby pollution sources.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　recognized as; overlooked; dearth
affect
affect: standard unit headword
#435 TC DOUBLE
There is no (i) __________ in developing a scientific theory that is (ii) __________; rather, the generation, testing, and frequently, discarding of new ideas must be regarded as an essential part of the process by which scientists gain the knowledge and understanding they seek.There is no (i) __________ in developing a scientific theory that is (ii) __________; rather, the generation, testing, and frequently, discarding of new ideas must be regarded as an essential part of the process by which scientists gain the knowledge and understanding they seek.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　shame; eventually refuted
overly
overly: standard unit headword
#436 TC TRIPLE
When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　ambiguity; misleading; veracity
brevity
brevity: standard unit headword
#437 TC DOUBLE
The company CEO's easygoing manner (i) __________ his (ii) __________ the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.The company CEO's easygoing manner (i) __________ his (ii) __________ the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on
amplify
amplify: standard unit headword
#438 TC TRIPLE
For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　unorthodox; aloof from; strongly ahistorical
argumentconversantoverly
argument: standard unit headword | conversant: standard unit headword | overly: standard unit headword
#439 TC DOUBLE
Some historians of science maintain that the conceptual gap between successive scientific paradigms is so (i) __________ that scientists adhering to the new paradigm find it quite (ii) __________ to understand what their predecessors could have been thinking.Some historians of science maintain that the conceptual gap between successive scientific paradigms is so (i) __________ that scientists adhering to the new paradigm find it quite (ii) __________ to understand what their predecessors could have been thinking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　great; impossible

Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　banal; novelty

There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that almost require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new research make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that almost require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell

Blank 2

Because the requerimiento, a formal statement in Spanish that was read to indigenous people in Southwestern pueblos during the Coronado expedition in the 1540s, was replete with concepts and terms that (i) __________ the pueblo world of that day, its audience likely found it (ii) __________.Because the requerimiento, a formal statement in Spanish that was read to indigenous people in Southwestern pueblos during the Coronado expedition in the 1540s, was replete with concepts and terms that (i) __________ the pueblo world of that day, its audience likely found it (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　lacked equivalents in; inscrutable
underscore
underscore: standard unit headword
#448 TC TRIPLE
A minor criticism of the book, which is (i)__________ an understanding of the difficulty of doing direct research in Hong Kong's, is that So relied on secondary sources to tell the story of Hong Kong's political development, with previous histories of the period (ii)__________ his research. Given So's (iii)__________ many of the players in Hong Kong politics, it is surprising that he did not use interviews and other forms of direct research to delve further into the motivations, strategies, and tactics of participants.A minor criticism of the book, which is (i)__________ an understanding of the difficulty of doing direct research in Hong Kong's, is that So relied on secondary sources to tell the story of Hong Kong's political development, with previous histories of the period (ii)__________ his research. Given So's (iii)__________ many of the players in Hong Kong politics, it is surprising that he did not use interviews and other forms of direct research to delve further into the motivations, strategies, and tactics of participants.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　tempered by; playing a large role in; proximity to

deference: standard unit headword | criticism: standard unit headword
#449 TC DOUBLE
Testing alternate versions of web pages in real time can make Internet-based companies (i) __________ some user preferences, but that does not necessarily make those companies (ii) __________, as 10,000 ongoing tweaks to the company web site do not add up to a fundamental change of direction when one is needed.Testing alternate versions of web pages in real time can make Internet-based companies (i) __________ some user preferences, but that does not necessarily make those companies (ii) __________, as 10,000 ongoing tweaks to the company web site do not add up to a fundamental change of direction when one is needed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　rapidly responsive to; agile

#450 TC TRIPLE
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　temptation; occlude

Blank 1

Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　appreciated; kept them at a distance; contributing to

Blank 2
答案：B, F　deviates from; caricature

#454 TC TRIPLE
The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　anonymity; unable to establish; dismiss

Blank 2
答案：A, E　neglects; problematic
